name: code-instructor
description: Educational code development skill that teaches programming concepts while building applications. Use when the user wants to learn how code works, understand programming concepts, or build an app with detailed explanations. Provides line-by-line breakdowns, explains the 'why' behind code patterns, uses pedagogical teaching methods, and builds apps incrementally with educational commentary at each step.
capabilities: Provides step-by-step code explanations with line-by-line breakdowns
Uses progressive complexity to introduce concepts from simple to advanced
Identifies and explains common beginner mistakes with corrections
Adapts teaching style based on learner experience level
Creates educational code annotations for teaching purposes
author: Emz1998
securitySummary: This educational skill contains only documentation and teaching references with code examples. All static findings are false positives caused by the scanner misidentifying markdown documentation code blocks as executable commands. The skill poses no security risk to users.
valueStatement: New programmers often struggle to understand code through documentation alone. This skill transforms learning into interactive app-building sessions where every line is explained, concepts are introduced progressively, and learning happens through practical doing.
userTitle: Learn Code by Building Real Apps
limitations: Does not execute or run code - only explains and guides
Cannot access external files or repositories for learning
Relies on user to implement code in their own environment
Does not provide automated testing or validation

useCases.targetUser: Beginner programmers learning their first language
useCases.title: Beginner Learning Path
useCases.description: A new programmer wants to learn Python by building a todo list app. The skill walks them through creating a working application from scratch, explaining each concept before introducing the next.

useCases.targetUser: Developers seeking clarification on specific programming concepts
useCases.title: Concept Clarification
useCases.description: A developer is confused about async/await in JavaScript. The skill explains the concept using analogies, shows common mistakes, and guides them through building an async data fetching example.

useCases.targetUser: Educators and mentors teaching programming concepts
useCases.title: Code Review Training
useCases.description: A team lead wants to help junior developers understand React state management. The skill builds a React component incrementally, explaining why immutability matters and how state updates work.

promptTemplates.title: Build a Simple App
promptTemplates.prompt: I want to learn how to build a [APP TYPE] in [LANGUAGE]. Start from scratch and explain every line of code as we go. Teach me the concepts step by step.
promptTemplates.scenario: When a user wants to build something new and learn simultaneously

promptTemplates.title: Explain This Concept
promptTemplates.prompt: I don't understand [CONCEPT NAME]. Explain it to me like I'm a beginner, show me a simple example, and then help me build something that uses it.
promptTemplates.scenario: When a user needs clarification on a specific programming concept

promptTemplates.title: Debug and Learn
promptTemplates.prompt: I made a mistake in my code: [DESCRIBE ERROR]. Instead of just fixing it, can you explain what went wrong, why it happens, and how to avoid it in the future?
promptTemplates.scenario: When a user encounters an error and wants to learn from it

promptTemplates.title: Progressive Enhancement
promptTemplates.prompt: We built [PREVIOUS PROJECT] using [BASIC APPROACH]. Now show me how to add [ADVANCED FEATURE] using better patterns and explain why those patterns are preferable.
promptTemplates.scenario: When a user wants to advance from basic to intermediate/advanced patterns

bestPractices: Start every teaching session by setting expectations and outlining what the learner will build step by step
Always explain the 'why' behind code patterns, not just the 'how', to build conceptual understanding
Use progressive complexity: introduce simple working code first, then add features one at a time with explanations
antiPatterns: Throwing advanced concepts at beginners without building foundational understanding first
Providing large code blocks without explanation, which overwhelms rather than teaches
Focusing only on syntax without connecting concepts to real-world applications

faq.question: What programming languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill works with any language but has specific guidance for Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, and React. The teaching methodology applies universally.

faq.question: Can this skill write code for me?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on teaching and explaining, not just generating code. Users implement code in their own environment while receiving guidance and explanations.

faq.question: How does the skill determine my experience level?
faq.answer: The skill adapts based on user questions and feedback. Signs of beginner status include basic syntax questions; signs of advancement include asking about optimization and patterns.

faq.question: Does the skill run the code I write?
faq.answer: No, the skill does not execute code. Users run code in their own environment. The skill provides explanations and guidance for writing and understanding code.

faq.question: How are mistakes handled in this skill?
faq.answer: Mistakes are treated as learning opportunities. The skill shows what went wrong, explains why it happens, demonstrates the fix, and generalizes the lesson.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for advanced topics?
faq.answer: Yes. While designed with beginners in mind, the skill can adapt to intermediate and advanced users by introducing patterns, best practices, and architectural discussions.
